_comment: Index of JSON Schema files for Nielsoln Rescue Toolkit module log outputs. Schema format: JSON Schema draft-07. All schemas use additionalProperties:true to allow forward-compatible new fields.

m01_persistence_scan.notes: Each line is one finding object. Validate line-by-line.

m05_disk_health.notes: Top-level is a JSON array, one object per block device.

m09_thermal_health.notes: Schema title: THERMAL_ANALYSIS. Top-level 'verdict' and 'timestamp' are passive/legacy fields. Preferred v2 fields: passive_verdict (idle sensors only), response_verdict (active load test — SKIPPED if not performed), overall_thermal_verdict (combined, use for reports), response_confidence, response_limitations. Limitation: Linux thermal policy under RescueZilla may differ from OEM firmware under Windows — active test results reflect live Linux behaviour, not Windows behaviour.

m17_system_summary.notes: sections: hardware, disk, thermal (v1 legacy/passive — idle sensor summary only, no load test), thermal_analysis (v2 preferred — includes passive_verdict/response_verdict/overall_thermal_verdict/temperature_rise_c/throttling_detected; use overall_thermal_verdict for reports), clamav, clamav_confidence, logon, persistence, services, software, upgrade, system_integrity, execution_surface, device_manager (missing_drivers/disabled_devices/problem_code_devices/missing_driver_files/old_drivers/unsigned_drivers/devices_by_class), cmos_health, storage_usage, disk_integrity, bad_sector_scan (conclusion_strength/coverage_percent/blocks_failed/coverage_limitation — SAMPLE_OK means clean sample only, not full clean bill of health).

m25_event_archive.notes: Run summary: single JSON file per run. Chunk lines: JSONL, validate each line separately.

m27_device_manager.notes: Schema title: DEVICE_MANAGER_FULL_STATUS. Adds service binding, driver files, compatible IDs, filter chains, decoded config flags/problem codes, present/disabled flags, System.evtx driver event correlation.

m48_bad_sector_scan.notes: Schema title: BAD_SECTOR_SCAN. Read-only sampled bad-sector/slow-read test. IMPORTANT: default profile is a sampled scan (~3% coverage). DO NOT interpret SAMPLE_OK as a full clean bill of health. Key fields: conclusion_strength (full/sampled/weak/unknown), coverage_percent, coverage_limitation (standard customer-facing limitation text), sampling_strategy, tested_ranges, blocks_attempted/failed/slow (canonical), sectors_* (legacy aliases). Safety block required: read_only=true, destructive=false, write_test_performed=false. Verdict enum includes SAMPLE_* prefix for sampled scans to prevent overclaiming. interpretation.customer_summary provides pre-written report text.
